violations.description: Preventing contamination from equipment, utensils, and wiping cloths. — --Cloth for wiping food spills was not stored in sanitizing solution (at proper concentration) between uses. Several used wiping clothes were left out in the bar and the kitchen prep areas.

Variance requirement for specialized processing methods. — --Food establishment did not obtain approved variance or HACCP plan from regulatory authority for special process of reduced oxygen packaging meats and other raw products.

Cleaning and maintenance of floors, walls and ceilings. — --Physical facilities (e.g. floors, walls, ceiling etc…) were not clean or not cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. The floors underneath the bar equipment/coolers need to be cleaned.

General employee practices. — --Food employee observed drinking/using open beverage container outside of designated area. Employees had open drink containers inside the food prep area.--Food employee observed eating while preparing food and outside of designated area. Observed employee in kitchen eating food while on the cook-line.

Preventing contamination during storage. — --Food item was not stored in a clean, dry location a minimum of 6 inches above the floor. Observed boxes of food stored in the walk-in freezer sitting on the floor.

Preventing contamination of packaged and unpackaged food. — --Raw animal food was not properly separated from raw or cooked ready-to-eat food. Raw chicken above portion cups of ranch and boxes of raw bacon stored over bags of soup.
Preventing contamination by consumers. — --Consumer self-service sign was not posted where needed or as required. No sign stating 1 use per place by the salad bar.

Handwashing lavatory supplies. — --Handwash lavatory was not supplied with supply of hand cleaning liquid, powder or bar soap. Soap was not provided for the handwashing sink on the west side of the bar.--Handwash lavatory was not supplied with supply of individual disposable towels, continuous towel supply system, or heated air-drying device. Paper towels were not provided for the handwashing sink on the west side of the bar.

Employee dressing areas and lockers. — --employees' clothing/possessions were in an area where contamination of food, equipment, utensils, linens or single-service items may occur. Employees coats and jackets were hanging off of cooking utensils in the back of the kitchen.

Storage and handling of maintenance items. — --Wet-use maintenance items, such as mops, were not placed in a position that allows proper air-drying. There was 1 mop that was not properly hung up to dry over the drain.
